cards.front: What is the purpose of using 'ROLLBACK' in SQL?
cards.back: To undo the changes made in the last operation, effectively reverting to a previous state

cards.front: Which of the following is a valid attribute in the EMPLOYEE table?
cards.back: EMPNO, ENAME, JOB, MANAGER_NO, SAL, COMMISSION

cards.front: How would you insert three records into the EMPLOYEE table?
cards.back: INSERT INTO EMPLOYEE (EMPNO, ENAME, JOB, MANAGER_NO, SAL, COMMISSION) VALUES (EMPNO1, ENAME1, JOB1, MANAGER_NO1, SAL1, COMMISSION1), (EMPNO2, ENAME2, JOB2, MANAGER_NO2, SAL2, COMMISSION2), (EMPNO3, ENAME3, JOB3, MANAGER_NO3, SAL3, COMMISSION3)

cards.front: What happens when you insert a record into the EMPLOYEE table without specifying the columns?
cards.back: You will get an error, as SQL requires all columns to be specified in the INSERT statement

cards.front: Can you insert three records into the EMPLOYEE table using a single INSERT statement?
cards.back: Yes, you can use the 'INSERT INTO ... VALUES' statement to insert multiple records at once
